text.title: Documentation Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete failure modes when agents generate or maintain AGENTS.md documentation.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated files: agent lists files that do not exist in the directory.
antipattern.reason: LLMs confidently generate plausible-sounding file names from training data. AGENTS.md with non-existent paths causes downstream agents to issue failing Reads, breaking workflows.

antipattern.description: Length creep: agent expands AGENTS.md to 200+ lines with redundant prose and bullet lists.
antipattern.reason: Auto-loaded context that exceeds ~80 lines bloats every agent session. Enforce a hard 120-line cap; reject and regenerate if exceeded.

antipattern.description: ASCII art and box-drawing characters in AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: LLMs trained on README files default to fancy boxes and banners. These break on non-monospace terminals, waste tokens, and are explicitly forbidden by the project convention.

antipattern.description: Stale tables: file/endpoint/model tables not updated after a refactor.
antipattern.reason: Tables generated from AST or git ls-files stay accurate; hand-edited tables diverge within days. Regenerate from source, don't hand-edit after initial creation.

antipattern.description: Self-referential loop: agent reads its own previous draft as source of truth and treats hallucinations as fact.
antipattern.reason: Each generation pass must start from the raw repository state (git ls-files, actual file reads), never from a previous doc version. Previous docs are hypotheses, not ground truth.

antipattern.description: Multi-language confusion: in a monorepo with Python + TS + Go, the agent picks one language and ignores the others.
antipattern.reason: Pass per-package outlines scoped to each language subdirectory, not a whole-repo outline.

antipattern.description: Duplicating parent context: AGENTS.md for a subpackage repeats information already in the parent AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: Each AGENTS.md covers only its own directory. Parents are auto-loaded already; duplication wastes tokens and goes stale independently.
